title: Youth Employment Summer Success (Y.E.S.S.)
description: Gain hands-on experience working in City of Stockton departments including Public Works, IT, HR, Economic Development, and more. 100 paid spots for local youth.
requirements: Must live in Stockton city limits

title: City of Modesto Student Internship Program
description: Paid and volunteer internships across City of Modesto departments. Open to high school and accredited college students pursuing a degree. Up to 1,000 hours per year.
requirements: Enrolled in high school or accredited college/university

title: San Joaquin County College Internship
description: Work-based learning opportunities across San Joaquin County departments. Students gain professional experience in public administration, social services, planning, and more.

title: High School Internship Program
description: New in 2025! San Joaquin County launched a dedicated high school internship program to expose teens to careers in public service, administration, parks, and community health.
requirements: Enrolled high school student in San Joaquin County

title: Teen WorkNet Youth Program
description: Comprehensive career guidance, job readiness training, and paid work experience placements for youth facing barriers to employment. Locations in Stockton, Lodi, Manteca, and Tracy.
requirements: Must face employment barriers (low-income, foster youth, etc.)

title: Eckerd Connects Youth Employment Program
description: Employment and training services including career guidance, job placement, and paid work experience for in- and out-of-school youth in San Joaquin County.

title: Stanislaus County Student Internship
description: College students gain professional experience across Stanislaus County departments including health services, planning, HR, behavioral health, and public works.

title: E. & J. Gallo Winery Creative Services Internship
description: Work in Gallo's Creative Services team on brand design, marketing materials, and digital content for one of the world's largest wine producers, headquartered in Modesto.
requirements: Pursuing Bachelor's in Graphic Design, Marketing, or Communications

title: University of the Pacific Student Internship Program
description: UOP connects students with internship and co-op opportunities through its career center, including local Stockton employers, nonprofits, healthcare, and tech sectors. UOP students only.
requirements: Work with Career Center to find placement

title: CSU Stanislaus Career Internship Program
description: CSU Stanislaus connects students with regional employers for credit-bearing and non-credit internships across business, healthcare, education, social work, and STEM fields.

title: San Joaquin Delta College Work-Based Learning
description: Earn college credit while gaining real work experience through Delta College's Work-Based Learning program. Connects students to employers across the greater Stockton area.

title: STEP Program — Students with Disabilities Work Experience
description: The Student Transition and Employment Program (STEP) provides paid work readiness and on-the-job experience for students with disabilities aged 16–21 who have an active IEP or 504 plan.
requirements: Enrolled in school in SJ County

title: Summer Internship Program
description: 25 paid summer jobs across City of Tracy departments including City Manager's Office, Finance, Parks & Recreation, Police, and Public Works. Open to high school students living or attending school in Tracy.
requirements: Must live in or attend school in Tracy

title: Student Success Internship (SSI) Program
description: 30 undergraduate internship positions across UC Merced departments and community partners. Includes virtual and on-ground placements. Students apply through Handshake with a resume and cover letter.

title: Workplace Internship Network (WIN)
description: Merced College connects students who complete CTE Fast Track programs to paid internships with regional employers. Includes career readiness workshops before placement. Successful interns may be considered for full-time hire.

title: Merced Youth Jobs Program
org: City of Merced / Merced County Workforce Investment
description: Connects youth ages 16–24 to part-time employment, work readiness training, and a personal mentor. Participants earn up to $3,000 through 200 hours of paid work experience with local Merced-area employers.

title: Student Internship Program
description: Work on real projects at one of California's largest inland ports, with one day per week dedicated to career readiness and professional development. Students gain hands-on experience in port operations and logistics.
requirements: Interest in port operations, logistics, or public administration

title: Summer Youth Employment Program
pay: Paid (wages, taxes, and workers' comp covered)
description: Stanislaus County Workforce Development Board acts as employer of record — covering wages, taxes, and workers' compensation — while placing youth in summer jobs across the county. Includes pre-employment training.

title: Student Internship Program
description: Caltrans District 10, headquartered in Stockton, hires paid student interns to work alongside transportation professionals on California state highway projects. A strong pathway into state government and engineering careers.

title: Student Internship Program
org: CA Dept. of Food & Agriculture — Modesto Office
description: CDFA's Modesto field office offers flexible, year-round internships for college students to learn how California regulates agriculture and food systems. Open to all majors — not just agriculture students.

title: Clinical Education Internship
description: Sutter Health's Central Valley facilities offer clinical education internships for healthcare students in departments including ICU, Emergency, and Telemetry. A strong pathway to nursing and allied health careers in the 209.

title: Labor Summer Internship
org: UC Merced Community and Labor Center
description: UC Merced's Community and Labor Center places students with unions, worker centers, and community organizations for a paid 8-week summer internship focused on labor and community organizing in the Central Valley.
requirements: Interest in labor or community organizing

title: IT Internship Program
description: Gain hands-on experience in cybersecurity, client services, ERP systems, and more alongside seasoned City of Stockton IT professionals. Interns tackle real-world projects and receive mentorship in a supportive environment. Recognized by University of the Pacific as Cooperative Education Employer of the Year.
requirements: Interest in IT, cybersecurity, or related field
Agreement with City of Stockton required

title: Volunteer Internship Program
org: San Joaquin County District Attorney's Office
description: The SJ County District Attorney's Office is accepting volunteer internship applications for Summer 2026 and beyond from pre-law college students and law students (1L–4L). Interns gain exposure to prosecution, legal writing, and the criminal justice process working alongside deputy district attorneys.
requirements: Enrolled at a two- or four-year college or university (pre-law), OR currently in law school
Submit resume and cover letter to applications@sjcda.org
Law students must submit a writing sample

title: Volunteer Internship Program
description: The SJ County Probation Department seeks college interns to learn the criminal justice process firsthand. Interns assist with caseloads for juvenile and adult offenders, shadow deputy probation officers, and gain practical experience in monitoring clients and managing caseloads.
requirements: Currently enrolled at a two- or four-year college or university
Submit application, resume, and cover letter via sjcprobation.org

title: Free Code Academy + Paid Tech Internship
description: Bay Valley Tech offers a free 4-month coding bootcamp in Modesto followed by a 28-week paid tech internship at $25/hr. No prior coding experience required. Open to ages 16–30 in Stanislaus County (commuters considered). Funded by grants from education, government, and corporate partners.
requirements: Ages 16–30 in Stanislaus County (or willing to commute to Modesto)
Income limits may apply for those outside age range

title: 2026 Student Internship Program
description: Turlock Irrigation District (TID) offers paid 2026 student internships across multiple departments including Communications/Marketing, IT Services, Electrical Engineering, Civil/Geotechnical Engineering, and GIS. Interns work on real utility projects alongside seasoned professionals at one of California's largest irrigation districts.
requirements: Active college student or recent graduate
GPA of 2.0–3.0 or better (varies by role)

title: District Office Intern
org: CA State Senate — Office of Senator McNerney
description: Senator Jerry McNerney’s Stockton district office accepts paid intern applications on a rolling basis. Interns assist with constituent services, public outreach, and general office duties. A great entry point into California state government for students interested in public policy or law.

title: Fulfillment Area Manager Intern 2026
description: Amazon’s summer internship at their Tracy fulfillment center. Interns learn to manage teams of 50–100 associates, lead a real process-improvement project, and gain hands-on operations experience. Strong pathway to a full-time Area Manager role with one of the world’s largest employers. Relocation/housing assistance provided.
requirements: Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s program (Supply Chain, Business, Engineering, or related preferred)
Expected graduation between December 2026 and August 2027
Work authorization without employer sponsorship required

title: IT Program Analyst Internship 2026
description: Gallo’s award-winning IT department in Modesto is recruiting summer 2026 program analyst interns. Interns collaborate cross-functionally with Marketing, Sales, Finance, Manufacturing, and R&D teams, gaining exposure to enterprise technology at one of the world’s largest wine producers.
requirements: Pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s in Computer Science, MIS, Data Science, Statistics, Economics, or related field
Expected graduation between December 2026 and June 2028
Programming skills (Python, Java, or C++) preferred

title: IT Support Internship — Summer 2026
description: Harbor Foods, a fourth-generation family-owned food distributor headquartered in Modesto, is hiring an IT Support intern for Summer 2026. Interns assist the ITS1 and ITS2 teams with help-desk tickets, hardware/software troubleshooting, device deployments, and user documentation. Fully onsite at their Modesto distribution center.
requirements: Current Junior or Senior standing in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a directly related field
Must reside in the greater Modesto/Mid-Valley area
Intermediate to advanced Microsoft 365 knowledge preferred

title: Summer Internship Program
description: One of the world's largest dairy processors, located in Hilmar (Merced County), offers 10-week paid summer internships in operations, finance, quality, R&D, applications engineering, and IT. Interns work on real projects alongside experienced professionals and participate in dairy visits and leadership sessions.
requirements: Completed sophomore year at a college or university
Pursuing a relevant degree (Engineering, Food Science, Finance, IT, etc.)

title: Valley Legislative Internship Program
description: Work part-time in a California State Assembly or Senate office in the San Joaquin Valley — including Stockton, Modesto, Merced, and Fresno districts. Students gain hands-on experience in policy research, constituent services, and legislative process. $2,500 stipend paid upon completion. Open to students at CSU Stanislaus, UC Merced, Fresno State, and CSU Bakersfield.
requirements: Enrolled at CSU Stanislaus, UC Merced, Fresno State, or CSU Bakersfield
3.0 GPA or higher (requirement varies by campus)

title: Inclusive Interaction Lab High School Internship
description: Professor Ahmed Arif's research lab at UC Merced accepts four high school interns each summer to learn software development, ethical research practices, and real-world CS skills. Interns build software systems and receive direct mentorship from university faculty. Mostly virtual, open to high school students across California's Central Valley.
requirements: Interest in computer science or software development
Ability to commit 20 hrs/week for 12 weeks

title: Food Safety & Quality Assurance Intern
description: Gain hands-on experience in the laboratory and production areas of DFA's Turlock cheese and whey plant. Interns perform chemical and microbiological testing of finished products, assist with food safety programs including HACCP and sanitation, and write or revise Standard Operating Procedures.
requirements: Pursuing degree in Food Science, Biology, or Agricultural Science

title: Volunteer Internship Program
description: Structured volunteer work experience in community corrections with the Merced County Probation Department. Interns are assigned to units in the Adult and Juvenile Divisions, completing an orientation period before reporting to their placement. Ideal for students studying criminology, social work, or public administration.
requirements: Currently enrolled at a college or university

title: Supply Chain Internship – Operations
description: Join Dot Foods' Modesto distribution center for a paid summer internship in supply chain operations. Interns gain real-world experience in logistics, warehouse operations, and process improvement while building professional skills and industry connections.
requirements: Working toward a Bachelor's degree in Supply Chain, Logistics, Operations Management, or related business field
Ability to work minimum 350 hours during internship tenure

title: Viticulture Research Internship 2026
description: Support Gallo's viticulture research team in characterizing grapevine phenotypic traits including bud fruitfulness, cluster architecture, berry color, and canopy structure. Interns collect field data, tissue samples for genetic analysis, and fruit samples for maturity and compositional analyses at Gallo's Modesto-area ranch locations.
requirements: Enrolled in or completed a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Life Sciences, Agricultural Sciences, or Engineering (Ag Business also accepted)
Valid California driver's license and reliable vehicle
Social sciences degrees (psychology, sociology, etc.) not eligible

title: Cooperative Work Experience Education (CWEE)
description: MJC’s Cooperative Work Experience program lets students earn college credit while gaining paid or unpaid work experience in their field of study. The CWEE coordinator connects students with Stanislaus County employers across healthcare, business, tech, and public service. All majors welcome.
requirements: Have a job or internship related to your major, or work with CWEE to find one
Complete a learning objectives agreement with your employer

title: College Internship Program — Operations & Engineering
description: Foster Farms, one of the West Coast’s largest poultry processors with major operations in Livingston (Merced County), offers paid summer internships in food processing operations, quality assurance, engineering, and supply chain. Interns work on real plant-floor projects and gain exposure to large-scale food manufacturing.
requirements: Pursuing a Bachelor’s in Food Science, Agricultural Engineering, Mechanical or Industrial Engineering, or related field

title: Student Internship — Environmental & Planning
org: San Joaquin Valley Air Pollution Control District
description: The San Joaquin Valley Air Pollution Control District — regulating air quality across eight Central Valley counties — offers paid student internships in environmental science, public health, engineering, and community outreach. Interns assist with permit reviews, air monitoring, and regulatory compliance projects.
requirements: Enrolled college student in Environmental Science, Public Health, Engineering, or Policy
Must reside or study in the San Joaquin Valley region

title: Student Assistant — Delta Operations
description: The CA Department of Water Resources’ Stockton office oversees Sacramento–San Joaquin Delta water management and periodically hires Student Assistants for work in environmental science, hydrology, engineering, data analysis, and administration. A strong pathway into state water policy careers.
requirements: Currently enrolled at a California college or university
Pursuing degree in Environmental Science, Civil Engineering, Hydrology, or related field
Apply through CalCareers (CA state jobs system)

title: Medical Assistant Externship
description: Sutter Gould Medical Foundation — one of Stanislaus County’s largest medical groups — accepts Medical Assistant and clinical externship students from accredited programs. Externs rotate through primary care, specialty, and urgent care clinics in the Modesto area, applying classroom skills in a real clinical setting.
requirements: Enrolled in an accredited Medical Assistant or healthcare program
Program coordinator must initiate clinical placement
Background check and health screening required

title: Student Intern — Education Programs
description: SCOE — the regional education agency serving all Stanislaus County school districts — hires student interns in education, counseling, social services, HR, communications, and technology. Interns contribute to programs supporting K-12 students across the county.
requirements: Enrolled at a college or university
Pursuing degree in Education, Social Work, Psychology, Business, or related field

title: Youth Development Internship
description: The YMCA of San Joaquin County offers paid internships and program leader roles for young adults interested in youth development, after-school programming, aquatics, and community health. Interns gain supervised leadership experience while supporting programs serving thousands of Stockton-area youth annually.
requirements: Interest in working with youth and a positive attitude
CPR/First Aid certification (can be obtained after hire)

title: Nonprofit Work Experience Program
description: Goodwill Central Valley — with locations across Modesto, Stockton, and the region — provides subsidized work experience and career training for youth through WIOA-funded workforce programs. Participants gain skills in retail, logistics, customer service, or nonprofit operations, with a pathway to full-time employment.
requirements: Income or employment barrier eligibility (WIOA requirements)
Stanislaus or San Joaquin County resident

title: Automation Engineer Intern (Summer 2026)
description: Hands-on experience in Amazon fulfillment center automation. Learn to maintain and troubleshoot material handling control systems including PLC/PC controllers, industrial networks, motor control systems, and servo drives. Includes an intern project focused on continuous improvement.
requirements: Currently enrolled in bachelor's degree program (graduating Dec 2026–Aug 2027)
Interest in automation, electrical, or mechanical engineering
Able to stand/walk for 12-hour shifts and lift up to 49 lbs

title: Winemaking & Spirits Distillation Internship 2026
description: Assist with production winemaking at commercial scale or small-lot research production. Spirits distillation interns work with brandy, whiskey, rum, flavored vodkas, hard seltzers, and other spirits. Gain hands-on experience in one of the world's largest wineries.
requirements: Enrolled in relevant degree program (enology, food science, chemistry, or related)
Interest in winemaking or spirits production

title: Chemistry Research and Applications Internship 2026
description: Research internship focused on chemistry applications in the wine and spirits industry. Work alongside experienced scientists on analytical chemistry, quality assurance, and product development projects.
requirements: Enrolled in chemistry, biochemistry, or related science degree program

title: Product Insights & Innovation Internship 2026
description: Work on product innovation and consumer insights projects at Gallo. Gain cross-functional exposure to Marketing, Sales, Finance, Manufacturing, Winemaking, and R&D teams while contributing to real product development initiatives.
requirements: Enrolled in business, marketing, food science, or related degree program

title: Affordable Housing Development Internship 2026–2027
hours: Full-time summer (40 hrs/week), part-time fall/spring (16–20 hrs/week)
description: One-year paid internship working alongside experienced real estate development teams to help plan, finance, and build affordable rental housing in rural California communities. Gain insight into the full life cycle of housing development from predevelopment to completion.
requirements: College junior or senior (or recent graduate)
Interest in affordable housing, community development, or public policy
Able to commit to full-time summer and part-time academic year schedule

title: Geotechnical Engineering Internship (Summer 2026)
description: Comprehensive summer internship exploring geotechnical, geoenvironmental, hydrologic and geologic engineering consulting. Gain exposure to office, laboratory, and field experience in professional technical consulting at ENGEO's Lathrop office near Stockton.
requirements: Senior standing in civil engineering (preferred) or have completed soils introduction course
Interest in geotechnical, environmental, or water resources engineering

title: Volunteer Legal Internship
description: Individually structured volunteer internship at the Stanislaus County Public Defender's Office. Gain hands-on criminal defense experience including courtroom observation, legal research, and client interaction. Excellent preparation for aspiring trial attorneys.
requirements: Current law school student or recent law school graduate
Interest in criminal defense and public interest law
Email resume and cover letter to bitor@stancounty.com

title: STEP — Student Training Employment Program
description: Year-round program matching motivated young people ages 16–21 with paid work experience and job readiness training. Includes resume building, interview skills, and placement with local employers. Separate from the summer-only youth program.
requirements: Must meet eligibility requirements through Stanislaus County Workforce Development

title: Ecological Restoration Summer Internship
description: Assist with habitat restoration projects on the San Joaquin River. Interns collect and analyze plant and wildlife data, perform data entry, and complete a self-directed research project on riparian restoration.
requirements: Pursuing degree in biology, natural resources, or related field
Ability to work outdoors in temperatures exceeding 90°F

title: Manufacturing Intern — Stockton
description: Work in a fast-paced manufacturing environment at Niagara Bottling's Stockton plant. Interns apply continuous improvement methodologies and tackle hands-on projects in bottling operations.

title: Process Technology Scientist Internship 2026
description: Assist in all aspects of small-lot wine production at Livingston research wineries. Work hands-on from grape crush to bottling, gaining experience producing white, red, sparkling, and dessert wines.
requirements: Pursuing BS in Chemistry, Food Science, Biochemistry, Biology, or Microbiology

title: Winemaking & Spirits Distillation Internship 2026
description: Assist with production winemaking at commercial scale or spirits distillation (brandy, whiskey, rum, vodka) at Gallo facilities. Three tracks available: Commercial Winemaking, Research Winemaking, and Spirits Distillation.
requirements: Junior or Senior standing or graduate student
Pursuing degree in Enology, Food Science, Chemistry, or related field

title: Chemistry Research and Applications Internship 2026
description: Conduct chemistry research and applications work at Gallo's Modesto facility. Work alongside research scientists on analytical chemistry projects related to wine and spirits production.
requirements: Pursuing degree in Chemistry, Biochemistry, or related field

title: Finance Intern — Modesto
description: Support Finance and Sales Operations teams at G3 Enterprises. Work on analytics, reporting, and strategic recommendations while gaining hands-on experience in packaging and logistics industry finance.
requirements: Currently pursuing degree in Finance, Accounting, or Business

title: Finance Intern III
description: Work alongside accounting and operations personnel on capital project improvements. Create financial models, cash flow analyses, and present findings to the executive committee.
requirements: Pursuing degree in Accounting, Finance, or Business Analytics
Proficiency in Excel and financial software

title: Seasonal Internships (Marketing, Stadium Ops, Community)
description: Seasonal internship roles with the Stockton Ports minor league baseball team. Positions include Marketing & Community Assistant, Stadium Operations Assistant, and Fan Experience roles during the 2026 season.
requirements: Interest in sports management or marketing
Available for game days and events
